evals.prompt: Analyze the domain health of the codebase at /Users/finsterb/_git-os/ast-harvester. I want to understand the bounded contexts, any coupling problems, and get a full friction report.
evals.expected_output: Structured report with bounded contexts table, context map, domain event inventory, value stream, and friction report. Should identify the Config/Cloner/Parser/Enricher/Output as distinct concerns and flag the output.ts → enricher.ts dependency direction as a layer violation.

assertions.description: Output includes a Bounded Contexts section with a structured table or list mapping contexts to owning files
Output names a specific code entrypoint (e.g. harvest(), npm run harvest, index.ts) before tracing the value stream — not a hypothetical flow
Output correctly identifies that output.ts imports from enricher.ts (not the reverse) and flags this as a layer violation or missing ACL
Friction report items cite specific file names (not just module names) with a concrete fix direction

evals.prompt: Something feels off with the agentic-dev-team repo at /Users/finsterb/_git-os/agentic-dev-team — every time I add a new review agent I have to update like 5 different files. Where is all this coupling coming from? Give me a domain analysis.
evals.expected_output: Should identify all 5 registry locations by name and diagnose as distributed registry with no single source of truth. Should specifically identify the missing orchestrator Model Routing Table update in commands/agent-add.md steps 7-9.

assertions.description: Output names at least 4 of these 5 specific files as registry locations: CLAUDE.md (root), .claude/CLAUDE.md, docs/agent_info.md, agents/orchestrator.md, docs/team-structure.md

Output identifies the specific gap in commands/agent-add.md: steps 7-9 do not include updating the orchestrator Model Routing Table, meaning new agents fall back to their frontmatter model instead of the orchestrator-assigned one
Output includes a Context Map or context relationship section
Friction items suggest concrete fix directions naming specific files or architectural patterns

evals.prompt: We have a repo that contains only Terraform configs in infra/, GitHub Actions workflows in .github/workflows/, a Makefile, and some bash deploy scripts. There's no application code. Can you do a domain analysis on it?
evals.expected_output: Should return a brief skip note (under 50 lines) acknowledging there is no domain model. Should NOT produce any structured domain analysis under any label — including 'operational domain', 'infrastructure subdomains', 'concern boundaries', or equivalent reframings.

assertions.description: Output explicitly states there are no bounded contexts, aggregates, or domain model to analyze — not just a generic 'cannot help' refusal
Output does NOT produce a structured domain/operational/infrastructure analysis with subdomain boundaries, concern layers, or recommendation tables — regardless of how the sections are labeled
The response is fewer than 50 lines total
